item.title: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, far below forecasts
item.description: June's payroll gains were much lower than the 100,000 new hires that economists had predicted.

item.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: America 500? What the U.S. will look like in another 250 years
item.description: From space to healthcare and artificial intelligence, what could the next 250 years of the United States look like?

item.title: Venezuelans displaced by deadly earthquakes seek shelter in crowded tent encampments
item.description: Many displaced Venezuelans have no home to return to after devastating twin earthquakes tore buildings and families apart. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports from a tent encampment in Caracas.

item.title: New student loan rules take effect today. Here's what to know.
item.description: Loan revamp affects how much students and families can borrow to pay for college, as well as their repayment options.

item.title: Trump made over $1 billion on crypto ventures last year, disclosure shows
item.description: President Trump earned more than a billion dollars from crypto-related ventures alone last year, according to a financial disclosure, including from his meme coin business and his family's cryptocurrency firm.

item.title: Huge Russian strike on Ukraine's capital kills at least 17, officials say
item.description: Russia launched a massive attack on Ukraine's capital, killing at least 17 people and prompting President Zelenskyy to call for sped-up weapons support from his partners.

item.title: Remains of WWII pilot killed during spy mission identified after 82 years
item.description: U.S. Army Air Forces 1st Lt. Franklin H. McKinney disappeared after leaving China for a spy mission over Thailand in November 1944.

item.title: Zelenskyy says U.S. aid is "too slow" after deadly Russian strikes on Kyiv
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy spoke about conditions in Kyiv following deadly Russian strikes that killed at least 18 people, according to officials. CBS News' Aidan Stretch has more.

item.title: U.S.-Iran talks pause for ayatollah's funeral after claims of progress
item.description: Negotiations between Iran and the U.S. will pause for a week for the funeral of Iran's slain supreme leader, but Trump, and mediators, say there's been progress.

item.title: Celeb planners on how MSG could become Taylor Swift's perfect wedding venue
item.description: Two elite event designers shared their thoughts on how Taylor Swift's team might transform Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Couple that stunned NYC by climbing Empire State Building could face prison time
item.description: Daredevil climber couple Angelina Nikolau and Ivan Kuznetsov face a host of charges after their death-defying climb up the antenna of New York City's famed Empire State Building. CBS News' Tom Hanson has more details.

item.title: Security guard rescued from rubble 8 days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Hernan Gil was brought out on a stretcher as elated rescuers cheered and hugged each other. He was loaded into a waiting ambulance and driven away.

item.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
item.description: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angela Nikolau, 33.

item.title: Can you settle credit card debt before it goes to collections?
item.description: Worried about your card account going to collections? You may have more control over that debt than you think.

item.title: Dads of Camp Mystic victims push for safety laws 1 year after floods
item.description: The fathers of a camper and a counselor who died last July Fourth after flash floods swept through Camp Mystic in Texas reflect on the tragedy a year later.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: What a Brooklyn bodega reveals about the craze for an experimental weight-loss drug
item.description: It isn't approved by the FDA, but we found an experimental weight-loss drug called retatrutide for sale at a local convenience store.

item.title: Key details before Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce get married
item.description: Fans around the world are preparing for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. Here's what to know before what some are calling the wedding of the century. CBS News' Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: Gen Z opens up about divisions and what they think the future of the U.S. holds
item.description: In the "CBS Mornings" series "USA to Z," Gen Z, who are those born between 1997 and 2012, open up about stereotypes regarding their generation, political divides and the future of the U.S.

item.title: Louisville mayor reacts to blistering heat wave slamming the U.S.
item.description: Louisville Mayor Craig Greenberg joins CBS News to discuss the response and precautions underway as a massive heat dome affects millions across the U.S.

item.title: Trump downplays expense of upgrading Air Force One plane gifted by Qatar
item.description: President Trump took his first trip aboard the new Air Force One, an aircraft gifted by Qatar. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: "Here for the peptides": Unapproved weight-loss drug sold at Brooklyn bodega
item.description: Behind the counter of a convenience store in Brooklyn, a cashier rang up a tiny vial of a peptide the store advertised as retatrutide — an experimental weight-loss drug whose sale is generally prohibited by federal law. Here's what CBS News correspondent Adam Yamaguchi found.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes death toll spikes, details emerge about U.S. deportees reportedly trapped
item.description: The death toll in Venezuela continues to grow as rescue efforts continue, and earthquake survivors seek a way forward. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: USMNT moves ahead in FIFA World Cup
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Soccer Team is moving on in the FIFA World Cup as the global tournament gets tense. CBS News' Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Catholic Church excommunicates 6 bishops from ultra-conservative society
item.description: The Vatican says bishops from the ultra-conservative Catholic SSPX society were automatically excommunicated after ignoring Pope Leo's plea for unity.

item.title: Shark attack alerts for cellphones authorized by new federal law
item.description: President Trump has signed "Lulu's Law," which requires the FCC to allow emergency alert messages for shark attacks. It was inspired by shark attack survivor Lulu Gribbin.

item.title: Security questions after 2 people climb to the top of the Empire State Building
item.description: There's security questions after two Russian climbers made it to the top of the Empire State Building in New York City on Wednesday and appeared to get engaged. They also unfurled a pro-peace banner. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers arrested after apparently getting engaged at the top
item.description: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.
